{"key_event":"Reagan-era deregulation, union decline, and carceral expansion","reasoning":"State power remained overwhelming: deregulation and tax cuts reduced economic-regulatory and fiscal reach, but the Volcker Fed, defense buildup, immigration enforcement, and anti-drug/carceral expansion offset this with stronger coercive and national-security capacity. Society power declined modestly as organized labor, industrial communities, and heavily policed minority communities weakened, despite stronger conservative mobilization, civil-rights/legal advocacy, immigrant legalization, and successful checks such as Bork’s defeat and Iran-Contra oversight.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":8.5,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":9.3}
